summary: Verified KB search count fix across all three modified pages (KBGlobalSearch, KBSearch, KBCategory). UI displayed counts now match backend `total` field exactly for every scenario tested. Singular/plural ternary works (1 result vs N results). Filter-active path shows 'N of TOTAL' format correctly. Empty state still renders for no-results. No console errors. Result cards still render correctly post-fix.

per_category_search_singular.note: Confirms singular/plural ternary in KBSearch.jsx works

category_sub_module_count.note: Sub-module heading now driven by `total` not `docs.length`

verification_matrix.console_errors: None observed on any tested page

critical_code_review_comments: KBCategory.jsx line 158-159: Heading text 'N documents in "sub"' uses `total` from the API. The backend filters by sub when ?sub= is passed (verified — backend returns total=6 for sub=Workday Gateway). Correct.
KBSearch.jsx line 122-124: Meta line correctly switches between '${total} results' (no filter) and '${filtered.length} of ${total} results' (any filter active). Verified live.

Minor: KBSearch.jsx still client-side filters with `docs.filter(...)` for type/level/version. Since the API call is capped at limit=200, if a category ever exceeds 200 docs the type/version sidebar counts and filtered list would be incomplete. Not a regression from this fix (was always there), but flag for future scalability. Currently safe at preview scale.

KBGlobalSearch.jsx line 30-40 and KBCategory.jsx line 48-58 both have a small race: if the user types quickly and triggers two requests, only the latter is canceled implicitly by React reconciliation — there is no AbortController. Not in scope of this bug, but worth tracking.
test_credentials: Not required — KB search pages are public
context_for_next_testing_agent: Counting fix is solid on preview. All counts (global, per-category, category landing, sub-module) now use the backend `total` field instead of `docs.length`. Singular/plural ternary verified live (q=payroll in integration-platform returns 1 → UI says 'result' not 'results'). Filter-active path verified live ('1 of 4 results for hcm' after Level=advanced filter on core-hcm). Backend used: GET /api/kb/docs?... already returns `total` via db.kb_docs.count_documents(query) — no backend change was needed. Preview DB is small (~24 docs); the original user-reported '50 cap' bug cannot reproduce here, but the fix is structurally correct because UI now reads the same `total` field that backend computes from count_documents.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: three KB pages (KBGlobalSearch, KBSearch, KBCategory) were calling GET /api/kb/docs?... without limit (or with limit=50) and then displaying `docs.length` as the result count. Backend caps responses at limit=50 by default but always returns the full `total` via count_documents — UI was ignoring `total`. Fix: bumped limit to 200 (so the docs array also contains most categories' full set for client-side type/level filtering) and switched all three count displays to read `r.data.total` from the API response, falling back to docs.length only when total is missing. Verified: every UI count now matches `total` returned by the same endpoint.
